audience.description: The intended audience to receive authentication tokens for the service. The default value is https://azurehealthcareapis.com

authority.description:
The Azure Active Directory (tenant) that serves as the authentication authority to access the service. The default authority is the Directory defined in the authentication scheme in use when running this provider.
Authority must be registered to Azure AD and in the following format: https://{Azure-AD-endpoint}/{tenant-id}.

smartProxyEnabled.description: Enables the 'SMART on FHIR' option for mobile and web implementations.

allowCredentials.description: If credentials are allowed via CORS.

allowedHeaders.description: A set of headers to be allowed via CORS.

allowedMethods.description: The methods to be allowed via CORS.

allowedOrigins.description: A set of origins to be allowed via CORS.

maxAgeInSeconds.description: The max age to be allowed via CORS.

audience.description: The intended audience to receive authentication tokens for the service.

authority.description: The Azure Active Directory (tenant) that serves as the authentication authority to access the service.

smartProxyEnabled.description: Is the 'SMART on FHIR' option for mobile and web implementations enabled?

allowCredentials.description: Are credentials are allowed via CORS?

allowedHeaders.description: The set of headers to be allowed via CORS.

allowedMethods.description: The methods to be allowed via CORS.

allowedOrigins.description: The set of origins to be allowed via CORS.

maxAgeInSeconds.description: The max age to be allowed via CORS.

access.description: The access level for this rule. Possible values are: `rw`, `ro`, `no`.

anonymousGid.description: The anonymous GID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

anonymousUid.description: The anonymous UID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

filter.description: The filter applied to the `scope` for this rule. The filter's format depends on its scope: `default` scope matches all clients and has no filter value; `network` scope takes a CIDR format; `host` takes an IP address or fully qualified domain name. If a client does not match any filter rule and there is no default rule, access is denied.

scope.description: The scope of this rule. The `scope` and (potentially) the `filter` determine which clients match the rule. Possible values are: `default`, `network`, `host`.

submountAccessEnabled.description: Whether allow access to subdirectories under the root export? Defaults to `false`.

accessRules.description: One to three `access_rule` blocks as defined above.

access.description: The access level for this rule. Possible values are: `rw`, `ro`, `no`.

anonymousGid.description: The anonymous GID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

anonymousUid.description: The anonymous UID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

filter.description: The filter applied to the `scope` for this rule. The filter's format depends on its scope: `default` scope matches all clients and has no filter value; `network` scope takes a CIDR format; `host` takes an IP address or fully qualified domain name. If a client does not match any filter rule and there is no default rule, access is denied.

scope.description: The scope of this rule. The `scope` and (potentially) the `filter` determine which clients match the rule. Possible values are: `default`, `network`, `host`.

submountAccessEnabled.description: Whether allow access to subdirectories under the root export? Defaults to `false`.

cacheNetbiosName.description: The NetBIOS name to assign to the HPC Cache when it joins the Active Directory domain as a server.

dnsPrimaryIp.description: The primary DNS IP address used to resolve the Active Directory domain controller's FQDN.

dnsSecondaryIp.description: The secondary DNS IP address used to resolve the Active Directory domain controller's FQDN.

domainName.description: The fully qualified domain name of the Active Directory domain controller.

domainNetbiosName.description: The Active Directory domain's NetBIOS name.

password.description: The password of the Active Directory domain administrator.

username.description: The username of the Active Directory domain administrator.

groupFileUri.description: The URI of the file containing group information (`/etc/group` file format in Unix-like OS).

passwordFileUri.description: The URI of the file containing user information (`/etc/passwd` file format in Unix-like OS).

baseDn.description: The base distinguished name (DN) for the LDAP domain.

certificateValidationUri.description: The URI of the CA certificate to validate the LDAP secure connection.

downloadCertificateAutomatically.description: Whether the certificate should be automatically downloaded. This can be set to `true` only when `certificate_validation_uri` is provided. Defaults to `false`.

encrypted.description: Whether the LDAP connection should be encrypted? Defaults to `false`.

server.description: The FQDN or IP address of the LDAP server.

dn.description: The Bind Distinguished Name (DN) identity to be used in the secure LDAP connection.

password.description: The Bind password to be used in the secure LDAP connection.

searchDomain.description: The DNS search domain for the HPC Cache.

servers.description: A list of DNS servers for the HPC Cache. At most three IP(s) are allowed to set.

accessPolicyName.description: The name of the access policy applied to this target. Defaults to `default`.

namespacePath.description: The client-facing file path of this NFS target within the HPC Cache NFS Target.

nfsExport.description: The NFS export of this NFS target within the HPC Cache NFS Target.

targetPath.description: The relative subdirectory path from the `nfs_export` to map to the `namespace_path`. Defaults to `""`, in which case the whole `nfs_export` is exported.

networkInterfacePrivateIpAddresses.description: The private IPv4 address of the network interface. Changing this forces a new Dedicated Hardware Security Module to be created.

subnetId.description: The ID of the subnet. Changing this forces a new Dedicated Hardware Security Module to be created.

batchFrequencyInSeconds.description: Time interval at which blobs are written to storage. Value should be between 60 and 720 seconds. Default value is 300 seconds. This attribute is mandatory for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

connectionString.description: The connection string for the endpoint.

containerName.description: The name of storage container in the storage account. This attribute is mandatory for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

encoding.description: Encoding that is used to serialize messages to blobs. Supported values are 'avro' and 'avrodeflate'. Default value is 'avro'. This attribute is mandatory for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

fileNameFormat.description: File name format for the blob. Default format is ``{iothub}/{partition}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{DD}/{HH}/{mm}``. All parameters are mandatory but can be reordered. This attribute is mandatory for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

maxChunkSizeInBytes.description: Maximum number of bytes for each blob written to storage. Value should be between 10485760(10MB) and 524288000(500MB). Default value is 314572800(300MB). This attribute is mandatory for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

name.description: The name of the endpoint. The name must be unique across endpoint types. The following names are reserved:  `events`, `operationsMonitoringEvents`, `fileNotifications` and `$default`.

resourceGroupName.description: The resource group in which the endpoint will be created.

type.description: The type of the endpoint. Possible values are `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`, `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusQueue`, `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusTopic` or `AzureIotHub.EventHub`.

endpointNames.description: The list of endpoints which will be enriched.

key.description: The key of the enrichment.

value.description: The value of the enrichment. Value can be any static string, the name of the IoT hub sending the message (use `$iothubname`) or information from the device twin (ex: `$twin.tags.latitude`)

condition.description: The condition that is evaluated to apply the routing rule. If no condition is provided, it evaluates to true by default. For grammar, see: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-query-language.

enabled.description: Used to specify whether the fallback route is enabled.

endpointNames.description: The endpoints to which messages that satisfy the condition are routed. Currently only 1 endpoint is allowed.

source.description: The source that the routing rule is to be applied to, such as `DeviceMessages`. Possible values include: `RoutingSourceInvalid`, `RoutingSourceDeviceMessages`, `RoutingSourceTwinChangeEvents`, `RoutingSourceDeviceLifecycleEvents`, `RoutingSourceDeviceConnectionStateEvents`, `RoutingSourceDeviceJobLifecycleEvents`.

connectionString.description: The connection string for the Azure Storage account to which files are uploaded.

containerName.description: The name of the root container where you upload files. The container need not exist but should be creatable using the connection_string specified.

defaultTtl.description: The period of time for which a file upload notification message is available to consume before it is expired by the IoT hub, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 48 hours, and evaluates to 'PT1H' by default.

lockDuration.description: The lock duration for the file upload notifications queue, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 5 and 300 seconds, and evaluates to 'PT1M' by default.

maxDeliveryCount.description: The number of times the IoT hub attempts to deliver a file upload notification message. It evaluates to 10 by default.

notifications.description: Used to specify whether file notifications are sent to IoT Hub on upload. It evaluates to false by default.

sasTtl.description: The period of time for which the SAS URI generated by IoT Hub for file upload is valid, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 24 hours, and evaluates to 'PT1H' by default.

action.description: The desired action for requests captured by this rule. Possible values are  `Accept`, `Reject`

ipMask.description: The IP address range in CIDR notation for the rule.

name.description: The name of the filter.

condition.description: The condition that is evaluated to apply the routing rule. If no condition is provided, it evaluates to true by default. For grammar, see: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-query-language.

enabled.description: Used to specify whether a route is enabled.

endpointNames.description: The list of endpoints to which messages that satisfy the condition are routed.

name.description: The name of the route.

source.description: The source that the routing rule is to be applied to, such as `DeviceMessages`. Possible values include: `RoutingSourceInvalid`, `RoutingSourceDeviceMessages`, `RoutingSourceTwinChangeEvents`, `RoutingSourceDeviceLifecycleEvents`, `RoutingSourceDeviceConnectionStateEvents`, `RoutingSourceDeviceJobLifecycleEvents`.

keyName.description: The name of the shared access policy.

permissions.description: The permissions assigned to the shared access policy.

capacity.description: The number of provisioned IoT Hub units.

name.description: The name of the sku. Possible values are `B1`, `B2`, `B3`, `F1`, `S1`, `S2`, and `S3`.

allocationWeight.description: The weight applied to the IoT Hub. Defaults to 0.

applyAllocationPolicy.description: Determines whether to apply allocation policies to the IoT Hub. Defaults to false.

connectionString.description: The connection string to connect to the IoT Hub. Changing this forces a new resource.

location.description: The location of the IoT hub. Changing this forces a new resource.

capacity.description: The number of provisioned IoT Device Provisioning Service units.

name.description: The name of the sku. Currently can only be set to `S1`.

connectionToIpNotAlloweds.description: Specifies which Ip is not allowed to be connected to in current device group.

localUserNotAlloweds.description: Specifies which local user is not allowed to Login in current device group.

processNotAlloweds.description: Specifies which process is not allowed to be executed in current device group.

duration.description: Specifies the time range. represented in ISO 8601 duration format.

max.description: The maximum threshold in the given time window.

min.description: The minimum threshold in the given time window.

type.description: The type of supported rule type. Possible Values are `ActiveConnectionsNotInAllowedRange`, `AmqpC2DMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `MqttC2DMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `HttpC2DMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `AmqpC2DRejectedMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `MqttC2DRejectedMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `HttpC2DRejectedMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `AmqpD2CMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `MqttD2CMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `HttpD2CMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `DirectMethodInvokesNotInAllowedRange`, `FailedLocalLoginsNotInAllowedRange`, `FileUploadsNotInAllowedRange`, `QueuePurgesNotInAllowedRange`, `TwinUpdatesNotInAllowedRange` and `UnauthorizedOperationsNotInAllowedRange`.

acrAuthentication.description: Is Principal Authentication enabled for the ACR repository? Defaults to `true`.

agentSendUnutilizedMsg.description: Is Agent send underutilized messages enabled? Defaults to `true`.

baseline.description: Is Security related system configuration issues identified? Defaults to `true`.

edgeHubMemOptimize.description: Is IoT Edge Hub memory optimized? Defaults to `true`.

edgeLoggingOption.description: Is logging configured for IoT Edge module? Defaults to `true`.

inconsistentModuleSettings.description: Is inconsistent module settings enabled for SecurityGroup? Defaults to `true`.

installAgent.description: is Azure IoT Security agent installed? Defaults to `true`.

ipFilterDenyAll.description: Is Default IP filter policy denied? Defaults to `true`.

ipFilterPermissiveRule.description: Is IP filter rule source allowable IP range too large? Defaults to `true`.

openPorts.description: Is any ports open on the device? Defaults to `true`.

permissiveFirewallPolicy.description: Does firewall policy exist which allow necessary communication to/from the device? Defaults to `true`.

permissiveInputFirewallRules.description: Is only necessary addresses or ports are permitted in? Defaults to `true`.

permissiveOutputFirewallRules.description: Is only necessary addresses or ports are permitted out? Defaults to `true`.

privilegedDockerOptions.description: Is high level permissions are needed for the module? Defaults to `true`.

sharedCredentials.description: Is any credentials shared among devices? Defaults to `true`.

vulnerableTlsCipherSuite.description: Does TLS cipher suite need to be updated? Defaults to `true`.

curve.description: Specifies the curve to use when creating an `EC` key. Possible values are `P-256`, `P-256K`, `P-384`, and `P-521`. This field will be required in a future release if `key_type` is `EC` or `EC-HSM`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

exportable.description: Is this certificate exportable? Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

keySize.description: The size of the key used in the certificate. Possible values include `2048`, `3072`, and `4096` for `RSA` keys, or `256`, `384`, and `521` for `EC` keys. This property is required when using RSA keys. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

keyType.description: Specifies the type of key, such as `RSA` or `EC`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

reuseKey.description: Is the key reusable? Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

lifetimePercentage.description: The percentage at which during the Certificates Lifetime the action associated with this Trigger should run. Changing this forces a new resource to be created. Conflicts with `days_before_expiry`.

endInUnits.description:
The absolute end time boundary. Applies to Video on Demand (VoD).
For the Live Streaming presentation, it is silently ignored and applied when the presentation ends and the stream becomes VoD. This is a long value that represents an absolute end point of the presentation, rounded to the closest next GOP start. The unit is defined by `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds`, so an `end_in_units` of 180 would be for 3 minutes. Use `start_in_units` and `end_in_units` to trim the fragments that will be in the playlist (manifest). For example, `start_in_units` set to 20 and `end_in_units` set to 60 using `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds` in 1000 will generate a playlist that contains fragments from between 20 seconds and 60 seconds of the VoD presentation. If a fragment straddles the boundary, the entire fragment will be included in the manifest.

forceEnd.description: Indicates whether the `end_in_units` property must be present. If true, `end_in_units` must be specified or a bad request code is returned. Applies to Live Streaming only. Allowed values: false, true.

liveBackoffInUnits.description:
The relative to end right edge. Applies to Live Streaming only.
This value defines the latest live position that a client can seek to. Using this property, you can delay live playback position and create a server-side buffer for players. The unit is defined by `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds`. The maximum live back off duration is 300 seconds. For example, a value of 20 means that the latest available content is 20 seconds delayed from the real live edge.

presentationWindowInUnits.description: The relative to end sliding window. Applies to Live Streaming only. Use `presentation_window_in_units` to apply a sliding window of fragments to include in a playlist. The unit is defined by `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds`. For example, set  `presentation_window_in_units` to 120 to apply a two-minute sliding window. Media within 2 minutes of the live edge will be included in the playlist. If a fragment straddles the boundary, the entire fragment will be included in the playlist. The minimum presentation window duration is 60 seconds.

startInUnits.description: The absolute start time boundary. Applies to Video on Demand (VoD) or Live Streaming. This is a long value that represents an absolute start point of the stream. The value gets rounded to the closest next GOP start. The unit is defined by `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds`, so a `start_in_units` of 15 would be for 15 seconds. Use `start_in_units` and `end_in_units` to trim the fragments that will be in the playlist (manifest). For example, `start_in_units` set to 20 and `end_in_units` set to 60 using `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds` in 1000 will generate a playlist that contains fragments from between 20 seconds and 60 seconds of the VoD presentation. If a fragment straddles the boundary, the entire fragment will be included in the manifest.

unitTimescaleInMiliseconds.description: Specified as the number of miliseconds in one unit timescale. For example, if you want to set a `start_in_units` at 30 seconds, you would use a value of 30 when using the `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds` in 1000. Or if you want to set `start_in_units` in 30 miliseconds, you would use a value of 30 when using the `unit_timescale_in_miliseconds` in 1.  Applies timescale to `start_in_units`, `start_timescale` and `presentation_window_in_timescale` and `live_backoff_in_timescale`.

label.description: A label that is assigned to a JobInputClip, that is used to satisfy a reference used in the Transform. For example, a Transform can be authored so as to take an image file with the label 'xyz' and apply it as an overlay onto the input video before it is encoded. When submitting a Job, exactly one of the JobInputs should be the image file, and it should have the label 'xyz'.

name.description: The name of the input Asset. Changing this forces a new Media Job to be created.

label.description: A label that is assigned to a JobOutput in order to help uniquely identify it. This is useful when your Transform has more than one TransformOutput, whereby your Job has more than one JobOutput. In such cases, when you submit the Job, you will add two or more JobOutputs, in the same order as TransformOutputs in the Transform. Subsequently, when you retrieve the Job, either through events or on a GET request, you can use the label to easily identify the JobOutput. If a label is not provided, a default value of '{presetName}_{outputIndex}' will be used, where the preset name is the name of the preset in the corresponding TransformOutput and the output index is the relative index of the this JobOutput within the Job. Note that this index is the same as the relative index of the corresponding TransformOutput within its Transform.

keyFrameInterval.description: Use an `ISO 8601` time value between 0.5 to 20 seconds to specify the output fragment length for the video and audio tracks of an encoding live event. For example, use `PT2S` to indicate 2 seconds. For the video track it also defines the key frame interval, or the length of a GoP (group of pictures). If this value is not set for an encoding live event, the fragment duration defaults to 2 seconds. The value cannot be set for pass-through live events.

presetName.description: The optional encoding preset name, used when `type` is not `None`. If the `type` is set to `Standard`, then the default preset name is `Default720p`. Else if the `type` is set to `Premium1080p`, the default preset is `Default1080p`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

stretchMode.description: Specifies how the input video will be resized to fit the desired output resolution(s). Allowed values are `None`, `AutoFit` or `AutoSize`. Default is `None`.

type.description: Live event type. Allowed values are `None`, `Premium1080p` or `Standard`. When set to `None`, the service simply passes through the incoming video and audio layer(s) to the output. When `type` is set to `Standard` or `Premium1080p`, a live encoder transcodes the incoming stream into multiple bitrates or layers. Defaults to `None`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

accessToken.description: A UUID in string form to uniquely identify the stream. If omitted, the service will generate a unique value. Changing this forces a new value to be created.

keyFrameIntervalDuration.description: ISO 8601 time duration of the key frame interval duration of the input. This value sets the `EXT-X-TARGETDURATION` property in the HLS output. For example, use PT2S to indicate 2 seconds. This field cannot be set when `type` is set to `Encoding`.

streamingProtocol.description: The input protocol for the live event. Allowed values are `FragmentedMP4` and `RTMP`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

onErrorAction.description: A Transform can define more than one outputs. This property defines what the service should do when one output fails - either continue to produce other outputs, or, stop the other outputs. The overall Job state will not reflect failures of outputs that are specified with `ContinueJob`. Possibles value are `StopProcessingJob` or `ContinueJob`.

relativePriority.description: Sets the relative priority of the TransformOutputs within a Transform. This sets the priority that the service uses for processing Transform Outputs. Possibles value are `High`, `Normal` or `Low`.

audioAnalysisMode.description: Possibles value are `Basic` or `Standard`. Determines the set of audio analysis operations to be performed.

audioLanguage.description: The language for the audio payload in the input using the BCP-47 format of 'language tag-region' (e.g: 'en-US'). If you know the language of your content, it is recommended that you specify it. The language must be specified explicitly for AudioAnalysisMode:Basic, since automatic language detection is not included in basic mode. If the language isn't specified, automatic language detection will choose the first language detected and process with the selected language for the duration of the file. It does not currently support dynamically switching between languages after the first language is detected. The automatic detection works best with audio recordings with clearly discernable speech. If automatic detection fails to find the language, transcription would fallback to 'en-US'." The list of supported languages is available here: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2109463.

presetName.description: The built-in preset to be used for encoding videos. The allowed values are `AACGoodQualityAudio`, `AdaptiveStreaming`,`ContentAwareEncoding`, `ContentAwareEncodingExperimental`,`CopyAllBitrateNonInterleaved`, `H264MultipleBitrate1080p`,`H264MultipleBitrate720p`, `H264MultipleBitrateSD`,`H264SingleBitrate1080p`, `H264SingleBitrate720p` and `H264SingleBitrateSD`.

analysisResolution.description: Possibles value are `SourceResolution` or `StandardDefinition`. Specifies the maximum resolution at which your video is analyzed. The default behavior is `SourceResolution` which will keep the input video at its original resolution when analyzed. Using `StandardDefinition` will resize input videos to standard definition while preserving the appropriate aspect ratio. It will only resize if the video is of higher resolution. For example, a 1920x1080 input would be scaled to 640x360 before processing. Switching to `StandardDefinition` will reduce the time it takes to process high resolution video. It may also reduce the cost of using this component (see https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/media-services/#analytics for details). However, faces that end up being too small in the resized video may not be detected.

audioAnalysisMode.description: Possibles value are `Basic` or `Standard`. Determines the set of audio analysis operations to be performed.

audioLanguage.description: The language for the audio payload in the input using the BCP-47 format of 'language tag-region' (e.g: 'en-US'). If you know the language of your content, it is recommended that you specify it. The language must be specified explicitly for AudioAnalysisMode:Basic, since automatic language detection is not included in basic mode. If the language isn't specified, automatic language detection will choose the first language detected and process with the selected language for the duration of the file. It does not currently support dynamically switching between languages after the first language is detected. The automatic detection works best with audio recordings with clearly discernable speech. If automatic detection fails to find the language, transcription would fallback to 'en-US'." The list of supported languages is available here: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2109463.

insightsType.description: Defines the type of insights that you want the service to generate. The allowed values are `AudioInsightsOnly`, `VideoInsightsOnly`, and `AllInsights`. If you set this to `AllInsights` and the input is audio only, then only audio insights are generated. Similarly if the input is video only, then only video insights are generated. It is recommended that you not use `AudioInsightsOnly` if you expect some of your inputs to be video only; or use `VideoInsightsOnly` if you expect some of your inputs to be audio only. Your Jobs in such conditions would error out.

resourceIds.description: A list of resource IDs of the given scope type which will be the target of action rule.

type.description: Specifies the type of target scope. Possible values are `ResourceGroup` and `Resource`.

recurrenceType.description: Specifies the type of suppression. Possible values are `Always`, `Daily`, `Monthly`, `Once`, and `Weekly`.

schedule.description: A `schedule` block as defined below. Required if `recurrence_type` is `Daily`, `Monthly`, `Once` or `Weekly`.

endDateUtc.description: specifies the recurrence UTC end datetime (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z').

recurrenceMonthlies.description: specifies the list of dayOfMonth to recurrence. Possible values are between `1` - `31`. Required if `recurrence_type` is `Monthly`.

recurrenceWeeklies.description: specifies the list of dayOfWeek to recurrence. Possible values are `Sunday`, `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday` and  `Saturday`.

startDateUtc.description: specifies the recurrence UTC start datetime (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z').

actionGroupId.description: The ID of the Action Group can be sourced from the `azure.monitoring.ActionGroup` resource.

webhookProperties.description: The map of custom string properties to include with the post operation. These data are appended to the webhook payload.

caller.description: The email address or Azure Active Directory identifier of the user who performed the operation.

category.description: The category of the operation. Possible values are `Administrative`, `Autoscale`, `Policy`, `Recommendation`, `ResourceHealth`, `Security` and `ServiceHealth`.

level.description: The severity level of the event. Possible values are `Verbose`, `Informational`, `Warning`, `Error`, and `Critical`.

operationName.description: The Resource Manager Role-Based Access Control operation name. Supported operation should be of the form: `<resourceProvider>/<resourceType>/<operation>`.

recommendationCategory.description: The recommendation category of the event. Possible values are `Cost`, `Reliability`, `OperationalExcellence` and `Performance`. It is only allowed when `category` is `Recommendation`.

recommendationImpact.description: The recommendation impact of the event. Possible values are `High`, `Medium` and `Low`. It is only allowed when `category` is `Recommendation`.

recommendationType.description: The recommendation type of the event. It is only allowed when `category` is `Recommendation`.

resourceGroup.description: The name of resource group monitored by the activity log alert.

resourceId.description: The specific resource monitored by the activity log alert. It should be within one of the `scopes`.

resourceProvider.description: The name of the resource provider monitored by the activity log alert.

resourceType.description: The resource type monitored by the activity log alert.

serviceHealths.description: A block to define fine grain service health settings.

status.description: The status of the event. For example, `Started`, `Failed`, or `Succeeded`.

subStatus.description: The sub status of the event.

events.description: Events this alert will monitor Possible values are `Incident`, `Maintenance`, `Informational`, `ActionRequired` and `Security`.

locations.description: Locations this alert will monitor. For example, `West Europe`. Defaults to `Global`.

services.description: Services this alert will monitor. For example, `Activity Logs & Alerts`, `Action Groups`. Defaults to all Services.

webhooks.description: One or more `webhook` blocks as defined below.

customEmails.description: Specifies a list of custom email addresses to which the email notifications will be sent.

sendToSubscriptionAdministrator.description: Should email notifications be sent to the subscription administrator? Defaults to `false`.

sendToSubscriptionCoAdministrator.description: Should email notifications be sent to the subscription co-administrator? Defaults to `false`.

serviceUri.description: The HTTPS URI which should receive scale notifications.

fixedDate.description: A `fixed_date` block as defined below. This cannot be specified if a `recurrence` block is specified.

name.description: Specifies the name of the profile.

recurrence.description: A `recurrence` block as defined below. This cannot be specified if a `fixed_date` block is specified.

rules.description: One or more (up to 10) `rule` blocks as defined below.

default.description: The number of instances that are available for scaling if metrics are not available for evaluation. The default is only used if the current instance count is lower than the default. Valid values are between `0` and `1000`.

maximum.description: The maximum number of instances for this resource. Valid values are between `0` and `1000`.

minimum.description: The minimum number of instances for this resource. Valid values are between `0` and `1000`.

end.description: Specifies the end date for the profile, formatted as an RFC3339 date string.

start.description: Specifies the start date for the profile, formatted as an RFC3339 date string.

timezone.description: The Time Zone of the `start` and `end` times. A list of [possible values can be found here](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/dn931928.aspx). Defaults to `UTC`.

days.description: A list of days that this profile takes effect on. Possible values include `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday` and `Sunday`.

hours.description: A list containing a single item, which specifies the Hour interval at which this recurrence should be triggered (in 24-hour time). Possible values are from `0` to `23`.

minutes.description: A list containing a single item which specifies the Minute interval at which this recurrence should be triggered.

timezone.description: The Time Zone used for the `hours` field. A list of [possible values can be found here](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/dn931928.aspx). Defaults to `UTC`.

dimensions.description: One or more `dimensions` block as defined below.

divideByInstanceCount.description: Whether to enable metric divide by instance count.

metricName.description: The name of the metric that defines what the rule monitors, such as `Percentage CPU` for `Virtual Machine Scale Sets` and `CpuPercentage` for `App Service Plan`.

metricNamespace.description: The namespace of the metric that defines what the rule monitors, such as `microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets` for `Virtual Machine Scale Sets`.

metricResourceId.description: The ID of the Resource which the Rule monitors.

operator.description: Specifies the operator used to compare the metric data and threshold. Possible values are: `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `LessThan`, `LessThanOrEqual`.

statistic.description: Specifies how the metrics from multiple instances are combined. Possible values are `Average`, `Min` and `Max`.

threshold.description: Specifies the threshold of the metric that triggers the scale action.

timeAggregation.description: Specifies how the data that's collected should be combined over time. Possible values include `Average`, `Count`, `Maximum`, `Minimum`, `Last` and `Total`. Defaults to `Average`.

timeGrain.description: Specifies the granularity of metrics that the rule monitors, which must be one of the pre-defined values returned from the metric definitions for the metric. This value must be between 1 minute and 12 hours an be formatted as an ISO 8601 string.

timeWindow.description: Specifies the time range for which data is collected, which must be greater than the delay in metric collection (which varies from resource to resource). This value must be between 5 minutes and 12 hours and be formatted as an ISO 8601 string.

name.description: The name of the dimension.

operator.description: The dimension operator. Possible values are `Equals` and `NotEquals`. `Equals` means being equal to any of the values. `NotEquals` means being not equal to any of the values.

values.description: A list of dimension values.

cooldown.description: The amount of time to wait since the last scaling action before this action occurs. Must be between 1 minute and 1 week and formatted as a ISO 8601 string.

direction.description: The scale direction. Possible values are `Increase` and `Decrease`.

type.description: The type of action that should occur. Possible values are `ChangeCount`, `ExactCount` and `PercentChangeCount`.

value.description: The number of instances involved in the scaling action. Defaults to `1`.

category.description: The name of a Diagnostic Log Category for this Resource.

enabled.description: Is this Diagnostic Log enabled? Defaults to `true`.

days.description: The number of days for which this Retention Policy should apply.

enabled.description: Is this Retention Policy enabled?

category.description: The name of a Diagnostic Metric Category for this Resource.

enabled.description: Is this Diagnostic Metric enabled? Defaults to `true`.

days.description: The number of days for which this Retention Policy should apply.

enabled.description: Is this Retention Policy enabled?

days.description: The number of days for the retention policy. Defaults to 0.

enabled.description: A boolean value to indicate whether the retention policy is enabled.

actionGroupId.description: The ID of the Action Group can be sourced from the `azure.monitoring.ActionGroup` resource

webhookProperties.description: The map of custom string properties to include with the post operation. These data are appended to the webhook payload.

componentId.description: The ID of the Application Insights Resource.

webTestId.description: The ID of the Application Insights Web Test.

aggregation.description: The statistic that runs over the metric values. Possible values are `Average`, `Count`, `Minimum`, `Maximum` and `Total`.

dimensions.description: One or more `dimension` blocks as defined below.

metricName.description: One of the metric names to be monitored.

metricNamespace.description: One of the metric namespaces to be monitored.

operator.description: The criteria operator. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `LessThan` and `LessThanOrEqual`.

skipMetricValidation.description: Skip the metric validation to allow creating an alert rule on a custom metric that isn't yet emitted? Defaults to `false`.

threshold.description: The criteria threshold value that activates the alert.

values.description: The list of dimension values.

alertSensitivity.description: The extent of deviation required to trigger an alert. Possible values are `Low`, `Medium` and `High`.

dimensions.description: One or more `dimension` blocks as defined below.

evaluationFailureCount.description: The number of violations to trigger an alert. Should be smaller or equal to `evaluation_total_count`.

evaluationTotalCount.description: The number of aggregated lookback points. The lookback time window is calculated based on the aggregation granularity (`window_size`) and the selected number of aggregated points.

ignoreDataBefore.description: The [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date from which to start learning the metric historical data and calculate the dynamic thresholds.

metricName.description: One of the metric names to be monitored.

metricNamespace.description: One of the metric namespaces to be monitored.

operator.description: The criteria operator. Possible values are `LessThan`, `GreaterThan` and `GreaterOrLessThan`.

skipMetricValidation.description: Skip the metric validation to allow creating an alert rule on a custom metric that isn't yet emitted? Defaults to `false`.

operator.description: The dimension operator. Possible values are `Include`, `Exclude` and `StartsWith`.

values.description: The list of dimension values.

actionGroups.description: List of action group reference resource IDs.

customWebhookPayload.description: Custom payload to be sent for all webhook payloads in alerting action.

emailSubject.description: Custom subject override for all email ids in Azure action group.

metricTrigger.description: A `metric_trigger` block as defined above. Trigger condition for metric query rule.

threshold.description: Result or count threshold based on which rule should be triggered.  Values must be between 0 and 10000 inclusive.

metricColumn.description: Evaluation of metric on a particular column.

threshold.description: The threshold of the metric trigger.    Values must be between 0 and 10000 inclusive.

name.description: Name of the dimension.

operator.description: Operator for dimension values, - 'Include'.

retentionInDays.description: Specifies the number of days to retain logs for in the storage account.

storageAccountAccessKey.description: Specifies the access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storageAccountAccessKeyIsSecondary.description: Specifies whether `storage_account_access_key` value is the storage's secondary key.

storageEndpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://MyAccount.blob.core.windows.net).

monthlyRetention.description: The monthly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 120 months. e.g. `P1Y`, `P1M`, `P4W` or `P30D`.

weekOfYear.description: The week of year to take the yearly backup in an ISO 8601 format. Value has to be between `1` and `52`.

weeklyRetention.description: The weekly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 520 weeks. e.g. `P1Y`, `P1M`, `P1W` or `P7D`.

yearlyRetention.description: The yearly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 10 years. e.g. `P1Y`, `P12M`, `P52W` or `P365D`.

retentionDays.description: Point In Time Restore configuration. Value has to be between `7` and `35`.

disabledAlerts.description: Specifies a list of alerts which should be disabled. Possible values include `Access_Anomaly`, `Sql_Injection` and `Sql_Injection_Vulnerability`.

emailAccountAdmins.description: Should the account administrators be emailed when this alert is triggered?

emailAddresses.description: A list of email addresses which alerts should be sent to.

retentionDays.description: Specifies the number of days to keep in the Threat Detection audit logs.

state.description: The State of the Policy. Possible values are `Enabled`, `Disabled` or `New`.

storageAccountAccessKey.description: Specifies the identifier key of the Threat Detection audit storage account. Required if `state` is `Enabled`.

storageEndpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://MyAccount.blob.core.windows.net). This blob storage will hold all Threat Detection audit logs. Required if `state` is `Enabled`.

useServerDefault.description: Should the default server policy be used? Defaults to `Disabled`.

results.description: A list representing a result of the baseline.

maxCapacity.description: The maximum capacity any one database can consume.

minCapacity.description: The minimum capacity all databases are guaranteed.

capacity.description: The scale up/out capacity, representing server's compute units. For more information see the documentation for your Elasticpool configuration: [vCore-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sql-database/sql-database-vcore-resource-limits-elastic-pools) or [DTU-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sql-database/sql-database-dtu-resource-limits-elastic-pools).

name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for this Elasticpool. The name of the SKU, will be either `vCore` based `tier` + `family` pattern (e.g. GP_Gen4, BC_Gen5) or the `DTU` based `BasicPool`, `StandardPool`, or `PremiumPool` pattern.

tier.description: The tier of the particular SKU. Possible values are `GeneralPurpose`, `BusinessCritical`, `Basic`, `Standard`, or `Premium`. For more information see the documentation for your Elasticpool configuration: [vCore-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sql-database/sql-database-vcore-resource-limits-elastic-pools) or [DTU-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sql-database/sql-database-dtu-resource-limits-elastic-pools).

loginUsername.description: (Required)  The login username of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

objectId.description: (Required) The object id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

tenantId.description: (Optional) The tenant id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

logMonitoringEnabled.description: (Optional) Enable audit events to Azure Monitor? To enable server audit events to Azure Monitor, please enable its main database audit events to Azure Monitor.

retentionInDays.description: (Optional) Specifies the number of days to retain logs for in the storage account.

storageAccountAccessKey.description: (Optional)  Specifies the access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storageAccountAccessKeyIsSecondary.description: (Optional) Specifies whether `storage_account_access_key` value is the storage's secondary key.

storageEndpoint.description: (Optional) Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://MyAccount.blob.core.windows.net).

principalId.description: The Principal ID for the Service Principal associated with the Identity of this SQL Server.

tenantId.description: (Optional) The tenant id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

type.description: Specifies the identity type of the Microsoft SQL Server. At this time the only allowed value is `SystemAssigned`.

emailSubscriptionAdmins.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the schedule scan notification will be sent to the subscription administrators. Defaults to `false`.

emails.description: Specifies an array of e-mail addresses to which the scan notification is sent.

enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if recurring scans is enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`.

encryptionEnabled.description: Enable or disable encryption for backups. Defaults to `false`.

encryptionPassword.description: Encryption password to use. Must be specified when encryption is enabled.

manualSchedule.description: A `manual_schedule` block as documented below. When this block is present, the schedule type is set to `Manual`. Without this block, the schedule type is set to `Automated`.

retentionPeriodInDays.description: Retention period of backups, in days. Valid values are from `1` to `30`.

storageAccountAccessKey.description: Access key for the storage account where backups will be kept.

storageBlobEndpoint.description: Blob endpoint for the storage account where backups will be kept.

systemDatabasesBackupEnabled.description: Include or exclude system databases from auto backup. Defaults to `false`.

fullBackupFrequency.description: Frequency of full backups. Valid values include `Daily` or `Weekly`. Required when `backup_schedule_automated` is false.

fullBackupStartHour.description: Start hour of a given day during which full backups can take place. Valid values are from `0` to `23`. Required when `backup_schedule_automated` is false.

fullBackupWindowInHours.description: Duration of the time window of a given day during which full backups can take place, in hours. Valid values are between `1` and `23`. Required when `backup_schedule_automated` is false.

logBackupFrequencyInMinutes.description: Frequency of log backups, in minutes. Valid values are from `5` to `60`. Required when `backup_schedule_automated` is false.

dayOfWeek.description: The day of week to apply the patch on.

maintenanceWindowDurationInMinutes.description: The size of the Maintenance Window in minutes.

maintenanceWindowStartingHour.description: The Hour, in the Virtual Machine Time-Zone when the patching maintenance window should begin.

keyVaultUrl.description: The azure Key Vault url. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

name.description: The credential name.

servicePrincipalName.description: The service principal name to access key vault. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

servicePrincipalSecret.description: The service principal name secret to access key vault. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

diskType.description: The type of disk configuration to apply to the SQL Server. Valid values include `NEW`, `EXTEND`, or `ADD`.

storageWorkloadType.description: The type of storage workload. Valid values include `GENERAL`, `OLTP`, or `DW`.

luns.description: A list of Logical Unit Numbers for the disks.

If `interval_in_seconds` is set to `30`, then `timeout_in_seconds` can be between `5` and `10`. The default value is `10`. If `interval_in_seconds` is set to `10`, then valid values are between `5` and `9` and `timeout_in_seconds` is required.

`ECP256`, `ECP384`, or `None`.

The IKE encryption algorithm. Valid

ikeIntegrity.description:
The IKE integrity algorithm. Valid
options are `GCMAES128`, `GCMAES256`, `MD5`, `SHA1`, `SHA256`, or `SHA384`.

The IPSec encryption algorithm. Valid

ipsecIntegrity.description:
The IPSec integrity algorithm. Valid
options are `GCMAES128`, `GCMAES192`, `GCMAES256`, `MD5`, `SHA1`, or `SHA256`.

The DH group used in IKE phase 2 for new child SA.

or `None`.

aadAudience.description:
The client id of the Azure VPN application.

This setting is incompatible with the use of

The STS url for your tenant

`aad_tenant`, `aad_audience`, `aad_issuer`, `root_certificate` and `revoked_certificate`.

revokedCertificates.description:
One or more `revoked_certificate` blocks which

rootCertificates.description:

used by the VPN clients to connect to the gateway.
This setting is incompatible with the use of
`aad_tenant`, `aad_audience`, `aad_issuer`, `radius_server_address`, and `radius_server_secret`.

requestMessage.description: A message passed to the owner of the remote resource when the private endpoint attempts to establish the connection to the remote resource. The request message can be a maximum of `140` characters in length. Only valid if `is_manual_connection` is set to `true`.

requestResponse.description:
Possible values are as follows:
Value | Meaning
-- | --
`Auto-Approved` | The remote resource owner has added you to the `Auto-Approved` RBAC permission list for the remote resource, all private endpoint connection requests will be automatically `Approved`.
`Deleted state` | The resource owner has `Rejected` the private endpoint connection request and has removed your private endpoint request from the remote resource.
`request/response message` | If you submitted a manual private endpoint connection request, while in the `Pending` status the `request_response` will display the same text from your `request_message` in the `private_service_connection` block above. If the private endpoint connection request was `Rejected` by the owner of the remote resource, the text for the rejection will be displayed as the `request_response` text, if the private endpoint connection request was `Approved` by the owner of the remote resource, the text for the approval will be displayed as the `request_response` text

ruleSets.description: A set of rules which evaluate upon event and data interception. This is defined in one or more `rule_set` blocks as defined below.

rules.description: One or more `rule` blocks as defined below.

expectedValue.description: A value that will be compared with the value in `property_path`.

reopenClosedIncidents.description: Whether to re-open closed matching incidents? Defaults to `false`.

healthCheckRetryTimeout.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, after which Service Fabric retries the health check if the previous health check fails. Defaults to `00:45:00`.

healthCheckStableDuration.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric waits in order to verify that the cluster is stable before it continues to the next upgrade domain or completes the upgrade. This wait duration prevents undetected changes of health right after the health check is performed. Defaults to `00:01:00`.

healthCheckWaitDuration.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric waits before it performs the initial health check after it finishes the upgrade on the upgrade domain. Defaults to `00:00:30`.

upgradeDomainTimeout.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric takes to upgrade a single upgrade domain. After this period, the upgrade fails. Defaults to `02:00:00`.

upgradeReplicaSetCheckTimeout.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric waits for a replica set to reconfigure into a safe state, if it is not already in a safe state, before Service Fabric proceeds with the upgrade. Defaults to `10675199.02:48:05.4775807`.

upgradeTimeout.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric takes for the entire upgrade. After this period, the upgrade fails. Defaults to `12:00:00`.

intervalInSeconds.description: The interval used to check the endpoint health from a Traffic Manager probing agent. You can specify two values here: `30` (normal probing) and `10` (fast probing). The default value is `30`.

path.description: The path used by the monitoring checks. Required when `protocol` is set to `HTTP` or `HTTPS` - cannot be set when `protocol` is set to `TCP`.

port.description: The port number used by the monitoring checks.

protocol.description: The protocol used by the monitoring checks, supported values are `HTTP`, `HTTPS` and `TCP`.

timeoutInSeconds.description: The amount of time the Traffic Manager probing agent should wait before considering that check a failure when a health check probe is sent to the endpoint. If `interval_in_seconds` is set to `30`, then `timeout_in_seconds` can be between `5` and `10`. The default value is `10`. If `interval_in_seconds` is set to `10`, then valid values are between `5` and `9` and `timeout_in_seconds` is required.

toleratedNumberOfFailures.description: The number of failures a Traffic Manager probing agent tolerates before marking that endpoint as unhealthy. Valid values are between `0` and `9`. The default value is `3`

azure:apimanagement/backend:Backend.description:
Manages a backend within an API Management Service.


## Import

API Management backends can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:apimanagement/backend:Backend example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/mygroup1/providers/Microsoft.ApiManagement/service/instance1/backends/backend1
```

apiManagementName.description: The Name of the API Management Service where this backend should be created. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

description.description: The description of the backend.

name.description: The name of the API Management backend. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

protocol.description: The protocol used by the backend host. Possible values are `http` or `soap`.

resourceGroupName.description: The Name of the Resource Group where the API Management Service exists. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

resourceId.description: The management URI of the backend host in an external system. This URI can be the ARM Resource ID of Logic Apps, Function Apps or API Apps, or the management endpoint of a Service Fabric cluster.

url.description: The URL of the backend host.

apiManagementName.description: The Name of the API Management Service where this backend should be created. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

description.description: The description of the backend.

name.description: The name of the API Management backend. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

protocol.description: The protocol used by the backend host. Possible values are `http` or `soap`.

resourceGroupName.description: The Name of the Resource Group where the API Management Service exists. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

resourceId.description: The management URI of the backend host in an external system. This URI can be the ARM Resource ID of Logic Apps, Function Apps or API Apps, or the management endpoint of a Service Fabric cluster.

url.description: The URL of the backend host.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering Backend resources.

description.description: The description of the backend.

url.description: The URL of the backend host.

azure:apimanagement/emailTemplate:EmailT.description:
Manages a API Management Email Template.


## Import

API Management Email Templates can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:apimanagement/emailTemplate:EmailTemplate example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/group1/providers/Microsoft.ApiManagement/service/instance1/templates/template1
```

apiManagementName.description: The name of the API Management Service in which the Email Template should exist. Changing this forces a new API Management Email Template to be created.

body.description: The body of the Email. Its format has to be a well-formed HTML document.

description.description: The description of the Email Template.

resourceGroupName.description: The name of the Resource Group where the API Management Email Template should exist. Changing this forces a new API Management Email Template to be created.

appId.description: The App ID associated with this Application Insights component.

applicationType.description: Specifies the type of Application Insights to create. Valid values are `ios` for _iOS_, `java` for _Java web_, `MobileCenter` for _App Center_, `Node.JS` for _Node.js_, `other` for _General_, `phone` for _Windows Phone_, `store` for _Windows Store_ and `web` for _ASP.NET_. Please note these values are case sensitive; unmatched values are treated as _ASP.NET_ by Azure. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

connectionString.description: The Connection String for this Application Insights component. (Sensitive)

dailyDataCapInGb.description: Specifies the Application Insights component daily data volume cap in GB.

azure:appplatform/springCloudCustomDomai.description:
Manages an Azure Spring Cloud Custom Domain.


## Import

Spring Cloud Custom Domain can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:appplatform/springCloudCustomDomain:SpringCloudCustomDomain example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/resGroup1/providers/Microsoft.AppPlatform/Spring/spring1/apps/app1/domains/domain.com
```

azure:appservice/certificate:Certificate.description:
Manages an App Service certificate.


## Import

App Service Certificates can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:appservice/certificate:Certificate example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/mygroup1/providers/Microsoft.Web/certificates/certificate1
```

appServicePlanId.description: The ID of the associated App Service plan. Must be specified when the certificate is used inside an App Service Environment hosted App Service. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

expirationDate.description: The expiration date for the certificate.

friendlyName.description: The friendly name of the certificate.

hostNames.description: List of host names the certificate applies to.

hostingEnvironmentProfileId.description: The ID of the the App Service Environment where the certificate is in use.
hostingEnvironmentProfileId.deprecationMessage: This property has been deprecated and replaced with `app_service_plan_id`

issueDate.description: The issue date for the certificate.

issuer.description: The name of the certificate issuer.

keyVaultSecretId.description: The ID of the Key Vault secret. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

name.description: Specifies the name of the certificate. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

password.description: The password to access the certificate's private key. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

pfxBlob.description: The base64-encoded contents of the certificate. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

resourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the certificate. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

subjectName.description: The subject name of the certificate.

appServicePlanId.description: The ID of the associated App Service plan. Must be specified when the certificate is used inside an App Service Environment hosted App Service. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

hostingEnvironmentProfileId.description: The ID of the the App Service Environment where the certificate is in use.
hostingEnvironmentProfileId.deprecationMessage: This property has been deprecated and replaced with `app_service_plan_id`

azure:appservice/certificateBinding:Cert.description:
Manages an App Service Certificate Binding.


## Import

App Service Certificate Bindings can be imported using the `hostname_binding_id` and the `app_service_certificate_id` , e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:appservice/certificateBinding:CertificateBinding example "/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/mygroup1/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/instance1/hostNameBindings/mywebsite.com|/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/mygroup1/providers/Microsoft.Web/certificates/mywebsite.com"
```

appServiceName.description: The name of the App Service to which the certificate was bound.

certificateId.description: The ID of the certificate to bind to the custom domain. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

hostname.description: The hostname of the bound certificate.

hostnameBindingId.description: The ID of the Custom Domain/Hostname Binding. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

sslState.description: The type of certificate binding. Allowed values are `IpBasedEnabled` or `SniEnabled`. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

certificateId.description: The ID of the certificate to bind to the custom domain. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

hostnameBindingId.description: The ID of the Custom Domain/Hostname Binding. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

azure:appservice/managedCertificate:Mana.description:
This certificate can be used to secure custom domains on App Services (Windows and Linux) hosted on an App Service Plan of Basic and above (free and shared tiers are not supported).

> NOTE: A certificate is valid for six months, and about a month before the certificate’s expiration date, App Services renews/rotates the certificate. This is managed by Azure and doesn't requre this resource to be changed or reprovisioned. It will change the `thumbprint` computed attribute the next time the resource is refreshed after rotation occurs, so keep that in mind if you have any dependencies on this attribute directly.


## Import

App Service Managed Certificates can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:appservice/managedCertificate:ManagedCertificate example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/resGroup1/providers/Microsoft.Web/certificates/customhost.contoso.com
```

expiryTime.description: The end time of the schedule.

interval.description: The number of `frequency`s between runs. Only valid when frequency is `Day`, `Hour`, `Week`, or `Month` and defaults to `1`.

monthDays.description: List of days of the month that the job should execute on. Must be between `1` and `31`. `-1` for last day of the month. Only valid when frequency is `Month`.

monthlyOccurrences.description: List of occurrences of days within a month. Only valid when frequency is `Month`. The `monthly_occurrence` block supports fields documented below.

weekDays.description: List of days of the week that the job should execute on. Only valid when frequency is `Week`.

azure:avs/privateCloud:PrivateCloud.description:
## Import

Vmware Private Clouds can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:avs/privateCloud:PrivateCloud example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/group1/providers/Microsoft.AVS/PrivateClouds/privateCloud1
```

> **NOTE :** `internet_connection_enabled` and `management_cluster.0.size` cannot be updated at the same time.

azure:batch/certificate:Certificate.description:
Manages a certificate in an Azure Batch account.


## Import

Batch Certificates can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:batch/certificate:Certificate example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/example-rg/providers/Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts/batch1/certificates/certificate1
```

accountName.description: Specifies the name of the Batch account. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

certificate.description: The base64-encoded contents of the certificate.

format.description: The format of the certificate. Possible values are `Cer` or `Pfx`.

name.description: The generated name of the certificate.

password.description: The password to access the certificate's private key. This can only be specified when `format` is `Pfx`.

publicData.description: The public key of the certificate.

resourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Batch account. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

thumbprint.description: The thumbprint of the certificate. At this time the only supported value is 'SHA1'.

displayName.description: The display name of this Batch Job. Changing this forces a new Batch Job to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Batch Job. Changing this forces a new Batch Job to be created.

priority.description: The priority of this Batch Job, possible values can range from -1000 (lowest) to 1000 (highest). Defaults to `0`.

taskRetryMaximum.description: The number of retries to each Batch Task belongs to this Batch Job. If this is set to `0`, the Batch service does not retry Tasks. If this is set to `-1`, the Batch service retries Batch Tasks without limit. Default value is `0`.

azure:batch/pool:Pool.description:
Manages an Azure Batch pool.


## Import

Batch Pools can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:batch/pool:Pool example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myGroup1/providers/Microsoft.Batch/batchAccounts/myBatchAccount1/pools/myBatchPool1
```

accountName.description: Specifies the name of the Batch account in which the pool will be created. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

autoScale.description: A `auto_scale` block that describes the scale settings when using auto scale.

certificates.description: One or more `certificate` blocks that describe the certificates to be installed on each compute node in the pool.

containerConfiguration.description: The container configuration used in the pool's VMs.

displayName.description: Specifies the display name of the Batch pool.

fixedScale.description: A `fixed_scale` block that describes the scale settings when using fixed scale.

maxTasksPerNode.description: Specifies the maximum number of tasks that can run concurrently on a single compute node in the pool. Defaults to `1`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

metadata.description: A map of custom batch pool metadata.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Batch pool. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

networkConfiguration.description: A `network_configuration` block that describes the network configurations for the Batch pool.

nodeAgentSkuId.description: Specifies the Sku of the node agents that will be created in the Batch pool.

resourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Batch pool. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

startTask.description: A `start_task` block that describes the start task settings for the Batch pool.

storageImageReference.description: A `storage_image_reference` for the virtual machines that will compose the Batch pool.

vmSize.description: Specifies the size of the VM created in the Batch pool.

azure:cdn/endpoint:Endpoint.description:
## Import

CDN Endpoints can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:cdn/endpoint:Endpoint example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/mygroup1/providers/Microsoft.Cdn/profiles/myprofile1/endpoints/myendpoint1
```

contentTypesToCompresses.description: An array of strings that indicates a content types on which compression will be applied. The value for the elements should be MIME types.

deliveryRules.description: Rules for the rules engine. An endpoint can contain up until 4 of those rules that consist of conditions and actions. A `delivery_rule` blocks as defined below.

geoFilters.description: A set of Geo Filters for this CDN Endpoint. Each `geo_filter` block supports fields documented below.

globalDeliveryRule.description: Actions that are valid for all resources regardless of any conditions. A `global_delivery_rule` block as defined below.

hostName.description: A string that determines the hostname/IP address of the origin server. This string can be a domain name, Storage Account endpoint, Web App endpoint, IPv4 address or IPv6 address. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

isCompressionEnabled.description: Indicates whether compression is to be enabled.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

name.description: The name of the origin. This is an arbitrary value. However, this value needs to be unique under the endpoint. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

optimizationType.description: What types of optimization should this CDN Endpoint optimize for? Possible values include `DynamicSiteAcceleration`, `GeneralMediaStreaming`, `GeneralWebDelivery`, `LargeFileDownload` and `VideoOnDemandMediaStreaming`.

originHostHeader.description: The host header CDN provider will send along with content requests to origins.

originPath.description: The path used at for origin requests.

origins.description: The set of origins of the CDN endpoint. When multiple origins exist, the first origin will be used as primary and rest will be used as failover options. Each `origin` block supports fields documented below.

probePath.description: the path to a file hosted on the origin which helps accelerate delivery of the dynamic content and calculate the most optimal routes for the CDN. This is relative to the `origin_path`.

profileName.description: The CDN Profile to which to attach the CDN Endpoint.

querystringCachingBehaviour.description: Sets query string caching behavior. Allowed values are `IgnoreQueryString`, `BypassCaching` and `UseQueryString`. `NotSet` value can be used for `Premium Verizon` CDN profile. Defaults to `IgnoreQueryString`.

resourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the CDN Endpoint.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

azure:cognitive/account:Account.description:
## Import

Cognitive Service Accounts can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:cognitive/account:Account account1 /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/group1/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/account1
```

customSubdomainName.description: The subdomain name used for token-based authentication. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

endpoint.description: The endpoint used to connect to the Cognitive Service Account.

fqdns.description: List of FQDNs allowed for the Cognitive Account.

kind.description: Specifies the type of Cognitive Service Account that should be created. Possible values are `Academic`, `AnomalyDetector`, `Bing.Autosuggest`, `Bing.Autosuggest.v7`, `Bing.CustomSearch`, `Bing.Search`, `Bing.Search.v7`, `Bing.Speech`, `Bing.SpellCheck`, `Bing.SpellCheck.v7`, `CognitiveServices`, `ComputerVision`, `ContentModerator`, `CustomSpeech`, `CustomVision.Prediction`, `CustomVision.Training`, `Emotion`, `Face`,`FormRecognizer`, `ImmersiveReader`, `LUIS`, `LUIS.Authoring`, `MetricsAdvisor`, `Personalizer`, `QnAMaker`, `Recommendations`, `SpeakerRecognition`, `Speech`, `SpeechServices`, `SpeechTranslation`, `TextAnalytics`, `TextTranslation` and `WebLM`. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

localAuthEnabled.description: Whether local authentication methods is enabled for the Cognitive Account. Defaults to `true`.

azure:compute/diskEncryptionSet:DiskEncr.description:
Manages a Disk Encryption Set.

> **NOTE:** At this time the Key Vault used to store the Active Key for this Disk Encryption Set must have both Soft Delete & Purge Protection enabled - which are not yet supported by this provider.


## Import

Disk Encryption Sets can be imported using the `resource id`, e.g.

```sh
 $ codeinfra import azure:compute/diskEncryptionSet:DiskEncryptionSet example /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/group1/providers/Microsoft.Compute/diskEncryptionSets/encryptionSet1
```

keyVaultKeyId.description: Specifies the URL to a Key Vault Key (either from a Key Vault Key, or the Key URL for the Key Vault Secret).

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Disk Encryption Set exists. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

name.description: The name of the Disk Encryption Set. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

resourceGroupName.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the Disk Encryption Set should exist. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Disk Encryption Set.

encryptionAtHostEnabled.description: Should all of the disks (including the temp disk) attached to this Virtual Machine be encrypted by enabling Encryption at Host?

extensionsTimeBudget.description: Specifies the duration allocated for all extensions to start. The time duration should be between `15` minutes and `120` minutes (inclusive) and should be specified in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to `90` minutes (`PT1H30M`).

healthProbeId.description: The ID of a Load Balancer Probe which should be used to determine the health of an instance. This is Required and can only be specified when `upgrade_mode` is set to `Automatic` or `Rolling`.

instances.description: The number of Virtual Machines in the Scale Set.

location.description: The Azure location where the Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set should exist. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

maxBidPrice.description: The maximum price you're willing to pay for each Virtual Machine in this Scale Set, in US Dollars; which must be greater than the current spot price. If this bid price falls below the current spot price the Virtual Machines in the Scale Set will be evicted using the `eviction_policy`. Defaults to `-1`, which means that each Virtual Machine in this Scale Set should not be evicted for price reasons.

name.description: The name of the Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

networkInterfaces.description: One or more `network_interface` blocks as defined below.

overprovision.description: Should Azure over-provision Virtual Machines in this Scale Set? This means that multiple Virtual Machines will be provisioned and Azure will keep the instances which become available first - which improves provisioning success rates and improves deployment time. You're not billed for these over-provisioned VM's and they don't count towards the Subscription Quota. Defaults to `true`.

rollingUpgradePolicy.description: A `rolling_upgrade_policy` block as defined below. This is Required and can only be specified when `upgrade_mode` is set to `Automatic` or `Rolling`.

scaleInPolicy.description: The scale-in policy rule that decides which virtual machines are chosen for removal when a Virtual Machine Scale Set is scaled in. Possible values for the scale-in policy rules are `Default`, `NewestVM` and `OldestVM`, defaults to `Default`. For more information about scale in policy, please [refer to this doc](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/virtual-machine-scale-sets-scale-in-policy).
